zh.lifeSummary: William Cramphorne是William Cramphorne和Mrs. Cycelie Cramphorne的兒子，於大約1520年出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。他於1560年7月21日與Jane Plowewrighte在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1605年2月1日在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年86歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Cramphorne是William Cramphorne和Mrs. Cycelie Cramphorne的儿子，于大约1520年出生在英国英格兰賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。他于1560年7月21日与Jane Plowewrighte在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1605年2月1日在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cramphorne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English crampe ‘curved’ + horn ‘horn’. Compare Crunkhorn .

variants.name: Crunkhorn
meanings: English: nickname: unexplained. Perhaps a variant of Cramphorn formed with Middle English crunkel(d) ‘wrinkle(d)’ rather than crampe, crumpe ‘curved, bent’. Compare Crankhorn, apparently similarly formed with crank ‘bent, crooked’: Thomas James Crankhorn, 1754 in IGI (Clerkenwell, Middx).

variants.name: Crampin
meanings: 2 English: nickname possibly from Middle English crampoun (Old French crampoun) ‘cramping iron, grappling iron’, possibly for a maker or user of such an implement.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: William Cramphorne was born about 1520, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of William Cramphorne and Mrs. Cycelie Cramphorne. He married Jane Plowewrighte on 21 July 1560, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 1 February 1605, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: William Cramphorne est né environ 1520, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de William Cramphorne et Mrs. Cycelie Cramphorne. Il a épousé Jane Plowewrighte le 21 juillet 1560, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 1 février 1605, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 86 ans.

it.lifeSummary: William Cramphorne è nato il circa 1520 nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di William Cramphorne e Mrs. Cycelie Cramphorne. Lui sposato Jane Plowewrighte il 21 luglio 1560, nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 1 febbraio 1605, nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, all'età di 86 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Cramphorneは約1520年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスでWilliam CramphorneとMrs. Cycelie Cramphorneの息子として生まれた。1560年7月21日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスでJane Plowewrighteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1605年2月1日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスで86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Cramphorne(은)는 대략 1520년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 William Cramphorne(와)과 Mrs. Cycelie Cramphorne의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1560년 7월 21일 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 Jane Plowewrighte(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1605년 2월 1일 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: William Cramphorne родился около 1520 г. в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали William Cramphorne и Mrs. Cycelie Cramphorne.
